Return of Credit Risk Premiums Not Used To Mitigate Losses, , , as amended by , , , provided that:Pub. L. 115–265, title II, § 212(d)132 Stat. 3749Pub. L. 117–58, div. B, title I, § 21301(j)(3)(D)135 Stat. 692Oct. 11, 2018Nov. 15, 2021“(1).—In generalNotwithstanding any other provision of law, the Secretary of Transportation, for purposes of , United States Code [see former ] (as in effect on the day before the amendments made by () took effect [])—section 22402 of title 4945 U.S.C. 822(f)(4)section 11607 of Public Law 114–94129 Stat. 1698Oct. 1, 2015“(A)not later than 30 days after the date of enactment of this Act [], and in consultation with the Director of the Office of Management and Budget, shall define the term ‘cohorts of loans’;Oct. 11, 2018“(B)before the deadline described in paragraph (2), shall return to the original source, on a pro rata basis, the credit risk premiums paid for the loans in the cohort of loans, with interest accrued thereon, that were not used to mitigate losses; and“(C)shall not treat the repayment of a loan after the date of enactment of [] as precluding, limiting, or negatively affecting the satisfaction of the obligation of its cohort prior to the enactment of .Public Law 114–94Public Law 114–94Dec. 4, 2015“(2).—Deadline describedThe deadline described in this paragraph is—“(A)if all obligations attached to a cohort of loans have been satisfied, not later than 60 days after the date of enactment of this Act; and“(B)if all obligations attached to a cohort of loans have not been satisfied, not later than 60 days after the date on which all obligations attached to the cohort of loans are satisfied.”

children.text: State and local governments;
entities implementing interstate compacts consented to by Congress under section 410(a) of the Amtrak Reform and Accountability Act of 1997 ( note);49 U.S.C. 24101
government sponsored authorities and corporations;
railroads;
entities participating in joint ventures that include at least 1 of the entities described in paragraph (1), (2), (3), (4), or (6);
limited option freight shippers that own or operate a plant or other facility, solely for the purpose of constructing a rail connection between a plant or facility and a railroad; and
private entities with controlling ownership in 1 or more freight railroads other than Class I carriers.

children.label: A
children.text: to acquire, improve, or rehabilitate intermodal or rail equipment or facilities, including track, components of track, cuts and fills, stations, tunnels, bridges, yards, buildings, and shops, and to finance costs related to those activities, including pre-construction costs;

children.label: B
children.text: to develop or establish new intermodal or railroad facilities;

children.label: C
children.text: to develop landside port infrastructure for seaports serviced by rail;

children.label: D
children.text: to refinance outstanding debt incurred for the purposes described in subparagraph (A) , (B), or (C);

children.label: E
children.text: to reimburse planning, permitting, and design expenses relating to activities described in subparagraph (A), (B), or (C); or

children.label: i
children.text: incorporates private investment of greater than 20 percent of total project costs;

children.label: ii
children.text: is physically connected to, or is within ½ mile of, a fixed guideway transit station, an intercity bus station, a passenger rail station, or a multimodal station, provided that the location includes service by a railroad;

children.label: iii
children.text: demonstrates the ability of the applicant to commence the contracting process for construction not later than 90 days after the date on which the direct loan or loan guarantee is obligated for the project under this chapter; and

children.label: iv
children.text: demonstrates the ability to generate new revenue for the relevant passenger rail station or service by increasing ridership, increasing tenant lease payments, or carrying out other activities that generate revenue exceeding costs.

children.heading: .—Operating expenses not eligible
children.label: 2 .—Operating expenses not eligible
children.text: Direct loans and loan guarantees under this section shall not be used for railroad operating expenses.
enhance public safety, including projects for the installation of a positive train control system (as defined in section 20157(i));
promote economic development;
enhance the environment;
enable United States companies to be more competitive in international markets;
are endorsed by the plans prepared under or chapter 227 of this title by the State or States in which they are located;section 135 of title 23
improve railroad stations and passenger facilities and increase transit-oriented development;
preserve or enhance rail or intermodal service to small communities or rural areas;
enhance service and capacity in the national rail system; or
would materially alleviate rail capacity problems which degrade the provision of service to shippers and would fulfill a need in the national transportation system.

children.heading: .—Extent of Authority
children.label: d .—Extent of Authority
children.text: The aggregate unpaid principal amounts of obligations under direct loans and loan guarantees made under this section shall not exceed $35,000,000,000 at any one time. Of this amount, not less than $7,000,000,000 shall be available solely for projects primarily benefiting freight railroads other than Class I carriers. The Secretary shall not establish any limit on the proportion of the unused amount authorized under this subsection that may be used for 1 loan or loan guarantee.

children.heading: .—Direct loans
children.label: 1 .—Direct loans
children.text: The interest rate on a direct loan under this section shall be not less than the yield on United States Treasury securities of a similar maturity to the maturity of the secured loan on the date of execution of the loan agreement.

children.heading: .—Loan guarantees
children.label: 2 .—Loan guarantees
children.text: The Secretary shall not make a loan guarantee under this section if the interest rate for the loan exceeds that which the Secretary determines to be reasonable, taking into consideration the prevailing interest rates and customary fees incurred under similar obligations in the private capital market.

children.heading: .—Authority of secretary
children.label: 1 .—Authority of secretary
children.text: In lieu of or in combination with appropriations of budget authority to cover the costs of direct loans and loan guarantees as required under section 504(b)(1) of the Federal Credit Reform Act of 1990, including the cost of a modification thereof, the Secretary may accept on behalf of an applicant for assistance under this section a commitment from a non-Federal source, including a State or local government or agency or public benefit corporation or public authority thereof, to fund in whole or in part credit risk premiums and modification costs with respect to the loan that is the subject of the application or modification. In no event shall the aggregate of appropriations of budget authority and credit risk premiums described in this paragraph with respect to a direct loan or loan guarantee be less than the cost of that direct loan or loan guarantee.

children.label: A
children.text: the circumstances of the applicant, including the amount of collateral offered, if any;

children.label: B
children.text: the proposed schedule of loan disbursements;

children.label: C
children.text: historical data on the repayment history of similar borrowers;

children.label: D
children.text: consultation with the Congressional Budget Office; and

children.label: E
children.text: any other factors the Secretary considers relevant.

children.label: A
children.text: The net present value of a future stream of State or local subsidy income or other dedicated revenues to secure the direct loan or loan guarantee.

children.label: i
children.text: tolls;

children.label: ii
children.text: user fees, including operating or tenant charges, facility rents, or other fees paid by transportation service providers or operators for access to, or the use of, infrastructure, including rail lines, bridges, tunnels, yards, or stations; or

children.label: iii
children.text: payments owing to the obligor under a public-private partnership.

children.label: C
children.text: An investment-grade rating on the direct loan or loan guarantee, as applicable, except that if the total amount of the direct loan or loan guarantee is greater than $150,000,000, the applicant shall have an investment-grade rating from at least 2 rating agencies on the direct loan or loan guarantee.

children.label: D
children.text: Revenue from projected freight or passenger demand for the project based on regionally developed economic forecasts, including projections of any modal diversion resulting from the project.

children.heading: .—Payment of premiums
children.label: 4 .—Payment of premiums
children.text: Credit risk premiums under this subsection shall be paid to the Secretary before the disbursement of loan amounts (and in the case of a modification, before the modification is executed), to the extent appropriations are not available to the Secretary to meet the costs of direct loans and loan guarantees, including costs of modifications thereof.

children.label: A
children.text: 60 days after the date of enactment of the Surface Transportation Investment Act of 2021 if the borrower has satisfied all obligations attached to such loan; or

children.label: B
children.text: if the borrower has not yet satisfied all obligations attached to such loan, 60 days after the date on which all obligations attached to such loan have been satisfied.

children.label: i
children.text: shall accept a net liquidation value of collateral; and

children.label: I
children.text: the market value of collateral; or

children.label: aa
children.text: inclusion in the pledge of all the assets necessary for independent operational utility of the collateral, including tangible assets such as real property, track and structure, motive power, equipment and rolling stock, stations, systems and maintenance facilities and intangible assets such as long-term shipping agreements, easements, leases and access rights such as for trackage and haulage;

children.label: bb
children.text: interchange commitments; and

children.label: cc
children.text: the value of the asset as determined through the cost or market approaches, or the market value of the going concern, with the latter considering discounted cash flows for a period not to exceed the term of the direct loan or loan guarantee.

children.label: i
children.text: adherence to the substance and principles of the Uniform Standards of Professional Appraisal Practice, as developed by the Appraisal Standards Board of the Appraisal Foundation; and

children.label: ii
children.text: the qualifications of the appraisers to value the type of collateral offered.

children.heading: .—Repayment of credit risk premiums
children.label: 7 .—Repayment of credit risk premiums
children.text: The Secretary shall return credit risk premiums paid, and interest accrued on such premiums, to the original source when all obligations of a loan or loan guarantee have been satisfied. This paragraph applies to any project that has been granted assistance under this section after the date of enactment of the Surface Transportation Investment Act of 2021.

children.label: A
children.text: 75 years after the date of substantial completion of the project;

children.label: B
children.text: the estimated useful life of the rail equipment or facilities to be acquired, rehabilitated, improved, developed, or established, subject to an adequate determination of long-term risk; or

children.label: i
children.text: 35 years; and

children.label: I
children.text: the difference between the estimated useful life and 35 years; multiplied by

children.label: II
children.text: 75 percent.
the direct loan or loan guarantee is justified by the present and probable future demand for rail services or intermodal facilities;

the applicant has given reasonable assurances that the facilities or equipment to be acquired, rehabilitated, improved, developed, or established with the proceeds of the obligation will be economically and efficiently utilized;
the obligation can reasonably be repaid, using an appropriate combination of credit risk premiums and collateral offered by the applicant to protect the Federal Government; and
the purposes of the direct loan or loan guarantee are consistent with subsection (b).

children.label: A
children.text: will not use any funds or assets from railroad or intermodal operations for purposes not related to such operations, if such use would impair the ability of the applicant, railroad, or railroad partner to provide rail or intermodal services in an efficient and economic manner, or would adversely affect the ability of the applicant, railroad, or railroad partner to perform any obligation entered into by the applicant under this section;

children.label: B
children.text: will, consistent with its capital resources, maintain its capital program, equipment, facilities, and operations on a continuing basis; and

children.label: C
children.text: will not make any discretionary dividend payments that unreasonably conflict with the purposes stated in subsection (b).

The Secretary shall not require an applicant for a direct loan or loan guarantee under this section to provide collateral. Any collateral provided or thereafter enhanced shall be valued as a going concern after giving effect to the present value of improvements contemplated by the completion and operation of the project, if applicable. The Secretary shall not require that an applicant for a direct loan or loan guarantee under this section have previously sought the financial assistance requested from another source.

children.label: A
children.text: the standards of section 24312, as in effect on , with respect to the project in the same manner that Amtrak is required to comply with such standards for construction work financed under an agreement made under section 24308(a); andSeptember 1, 2002

children.label: B
children.text: the protective arrangements established under section 22404, with respect to employees affected by actions taken in connection with the project to be financed by the loan or loan guarantee.

The Secretary shall require each recipient of a direct loan or loan guarantee under this section for a project described in subsection (b)(1)(F) to provide a non-Federal match of not less than 25 percent of the total amount expended by the recipient for such project.

children.heading: .—Application status notices
children.label: 1 .—Application status notices
children.text: Not later than 30 days after the date that the Secretary receives an application under this section, or additional information and material under paragraph (2)(B), the Secretary shall provide the applicant written notice as to whether the application is complete or incomplete.

children.label: A
children.text: provide the applicant with a description of all of the specific information or material that is needed to complete the application, including any information required by an independent financial analyst; and

children.label: B
children.text: allow the applicant to resubmit the application with the information and material described under subparagraph (A) to complete the application.

children.heading: .—In general
children.label: A .—In general
children.text: Not later than 60 days after the date the Secretary notifies an applicant that an application is complete under paragraph (1), the Secretary shall provide the applicant written notice as to whether the Secretary has approved or disapproved the application.

children.heading: .—Actions by the Office of Management and Budget
children.label: B .—Actions by the Office of Management and Budget
children.text: In order to enable compliance with the time limit under subparagraph (A), the Office of Management and Budget shall take any action required with respect to the application within that 60-day period.

children.heading: .—In general
children.label: A .—In general
children.text: Not later than 180 days after the date of enactment of the Surface Transportation Investment Act of 2021, the Secretary shall implement procedures and measures to economize and make available an streamlined application process or processes at the request of applicants seeking loans or loan guarantees.

children.label: i
children.text: seek a total loan or loan guarantee value not exceeding $150,000,000;

children.label: ii
children.text: meet eligible project purposes described in subparagraphs (A) and (B) of subsection (b)(1); and

children.label: iii
children.text: meet other criteria considered appropriate by the Secretary, in consultation with the Council on Credit and Finance of the Department of Transportation.

children.label: i
children.text: provide written notice to the applicant, including a justification for the delay and updated estimate of the time needed for approval or disapproval; and

children.label: ii
children.text: publish the notice on the dashboard described in paragraph (5).

children.label: A
children.text: the applicant type;

children.label: B
children.text: the location of the project;

children.label: C
children.text: a brief description of the project, including its purpose;

children.label: D
children.text: the requested direct loan or loan guarantee amount;

children.label: E
children.text: the date on which the Secretary provided application status notice under paragraph (1);

children.label: F
children.text: the date that the Secretary provided notice of approval or disapproval under paragraph (3); and

children.label: G
children.text: whether the project utilized the streamlined application process under paragraph (4).

children.label: i
children.text: the total value of the proposed loan or loan guarantee;

children.label: ii
children.text: the name of the applicant or applicants submitting the application;

children.label: iii
children.text: the proposed capital structure of the project to which the loan or loan guarantee would be applied, including the proposed Federal and non-Federal shares of the total project cost;

children.label: iv
children.text: the type of activity to receive credit assistance, including whether the project is new construction, the rehabilitation of existing rail equipment or facilities, or the refinancing an existing loan or loan guarantee;

children.label: v
children.text: if a deferred payment is proposed, the length of such deferment;

children.label: vi
children.text: the credit rating or ratings provided for the applicant;

children.label: vii
children.text: if other credit instruments are involved, the proposed subordination relationship and a description of such other credit instruments;

children.label: viii
children.text: a schedule for the readiness of proposed investments for financing;

children.label: ix
children.text: a description of any Federal permits required, including under the National Environmental Policy Act of 1969 ( et seq.) and any waivers under section 5323(j) (commonly known as the “Buy America Act”);42 U.S.C. 4321

children.label: x
children.text: other characteristics of the proposed activity to be financed, borrower, key agreements, or the nature of the credit that the Secretary considers to be fundamental to the creditworthiness review;

children.label: xi
children.text: the status of the application in the pre-application review and selection process;

children.label: xii
children.text: the cumulative amounts paid by the Secretary to outside advisors related to the application, including financial and legal advisors;

children.label: I
children.text: the factors used to determine risk for the proposed application;

children.label: II
children.text: an adjectival risk rating for each identified factor, ranked as either low, moderate, or high;

children.label: I
children.text: a range, based on the assessment of risk factors described in clause (xiii); or

children.label: II
children.text: a justification for why the estimate of the credit risk premium cannot be determined based on available information; and

children.label: xv
children.text: a description of the key information the Secretary needs from the applicant to complete the credit review process and make a final determination of the credit risk premium.

children.heading: .—Report upon request
children.label: B .—Report upon request
children.text: The Secretary shall provide the information described in subparagraph (A) not later than 30 days after a request from the applicant.

children.heading: .—Exception
children.label: C .—Exception
children.text: Applications processed using the streamlined application review process under paragraph (4) are not subject to the requirements under this paragraph.

children.heading: .—In general
children.label: 1 .—In general
children.text: The Secretary shall establish a repayment schedule requiring payments to commence not later than 5 years after the date of substantial completion.

children.heading: .—Accrual
children.label: 2 .—Accrual
children.text: Interest shall accrue as of the date of disbursement, and shall be amortized over the remaining term of the loan beginning at the time the payments begin.

children.heading: .—In general
children.label: A .—In general
children.text: If at any time after the date of substantial completion the obligor is unable to pay the scheduled loan repayments of principal and interest on a direct loan provided under this section, the Secretary, subject to subparagraph (B), may allow, for a maximum aggregate time of 1 year over the duration of the direct loan, the obligor to add unpaid principal and interest to the outstanding balance of the direct loan.

children.label: i
children.text: continue to accrue interest under paragraph (2) until the loan is fully repaid; and

children.label: ii
children.text: be scheduled to be amortized over the remaining term of the loan.

children.heading: .—Use of excess revenues
children.label: A .—Use of excess revenues
children.text: With respect to a direct loan provided by the Secretary under this section, any excess revenues that remain after satisfying scheduled debt service requirements on the project obligations and direct loan and all deposit requirements under the terms of any trust agreement, bond resolution, or similar agreement securing project obligations may be applied annually to prepay the direct loan without penalty.

children.heading: .—Use of proceeds of refinancing
children.label: B .—Use of proceeds of refinancing
children.text: The direct loan may be prepaid at any time without penalty from the proceeds of refinancing from non-Federal funding sources.

children.heading: .—In general
children.label: 1 .—In general
children.text: Subject to paragraph (2) and as soon as practicable after substantial completion of a project, the Secretary, after notifying the obligor, may sell to another entity or reoffer into the capital markets a direct loan for the project if the Secretary determines that the sale or reoffering has a high probability of being made on favorable terms.

children.heading: .—Consent of obligor
children.label: 2 .—Consent of obligor
children.text: In making a sale or reoffering under paragraph (1), the Secretary may not change the original terms and conditions of the secured loan without the prior written consent of the obligor.

children.heading: .—In general
children.label: 1 .—In general
children.text: Except as provided in paragraph (2), a direct loan provided by the Secretary under this section shall not be subordinated to the claims of any holder of project obligations in the event of bankruptcy, insolvency, or liquidation of the obligor.

children.label: i
children.text: the direct loan is rated in the A category or higher;

children.label: ii
children.text: the direct loan is secured and payable from pledged revenues not affected by project performance, such as a tax-based revenue pledge or a system-backed pledge of project revenues; and

children.label: iii
children.text: the program share, under this chapter, of eligible project costs is 50 percent or less.

children.heading: .—Limitation
children.label: B .—Limitation
children.text: The Secretary may impose limitations for the waiver of the nonsubordination requirement under this paragraph if the Secretary determines that such limitations would be in the financial interest of the Federal Government.

children.heading: .—In general
children.label: 1 .—In general
children.text: Subject to subsection (d) and paragraph (2) of this subsection, the Secretary may enter into a master credit agreement that is contingent on all of the conditions for the provision of a direct loan or loan guarantee, as applicable, under this chapter and other applicable requirements being satisfied prior to the issuance of the direct loan or loan guarantee.

children.label: A
children.text: establish the maximum amount and general terms and conditions of each applicable direct loan or loan guarantee;

children.label: B
children.text: identify 1 or more dedicated non-Federal revenue sources that will secure the repayment of each applicable direct loan or loan guarantee;

children.label: C
children.text: provide for the obligation of funds for the direct loans or loan guarantees contingent on and after all requirements have been met for the projects subject to the master credit agreement; and

children.label: D
children.text: provide 1 or more dates, as determined by the Secretary, before which the master credit agreement results in each of the direct loans or loan guarantees or in the release of the master credit agreement.

children.heading: .—Non-Federal Share
children.label: n .—Non-Federal Share
children.text: The proceeds of a loan provided under this section may be used as the non-Federal share of project costs for any grant program administered by the Secretary if such loan is repayable from non-Federal funds.
